Class VideoGame

  • java.lang.Object
    • VideoGame

  • public class VideoGame
extends java.lang.Object
    Drew Carrillo CEN 3024C 04/13/2026 CLASS NAME: VideoGame This class is the data model for our inventory system. It's basically just a container that holds info about a single video game. Role in the system: - Works with InventoryManager to pass game data around - Gets created when adding/updating games through the GUI - Uses encapsulation (private fields with getters/setters) so nothing gets messed up accidentally

    • Constructor Summary

      VideoGame(int gameID, java.lang.String title, java.lang.String platform, int releaseYear, double price, boolean isMultiplayer)
      Creates a new VideoGame object with all the game info.

    • Constructor Detail

      • VideoGame

        public VideoGame(int gameID,
                 java.lang.String title,
                 java.lang.String platform,
                 int releaseYear,
                 double price,
                 boolean isMultiplayer)
        Creates a new VideoGame object with all the game info. Purpose: Builds a complete game entry with validation so we don't end up with bad data (like negative prices or empty titles).
        Parameters:
        gameID - Unique identifier for the game (must be a positive number)
        title - Name of the game (can't be blank)
        platform - What system it's on like "PS5" or "PC" (can't be blank)
        releaseYear - When it came out (has to be between 1950 and 2026)
        price - How much it costs (can't be negative)
        isMultiplayer - true if it has multiplayer, false if it's single-player only
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if any of the inputs are invalid

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Returns a nicely formatted string with all the game info. This is what shows up in the text area and table.
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        A readable string with ID, title, platform, year, price, and multiplayer status
